tasks.title: Loot Choice after expedition is wonky
tasks.summary:
Loot recap shows the first item loot and ask to Bring this back or leave the rest. So user does not know which other loots are available before making a decision, and its bugged as right now "bring this back" saves the loot and moves to the next, effectively allowing user to save all loot in the end.

The intended result would be a carousel (can be animation less for now) of the available loots, have the user browse and then pick one. Put a confirmation msg that the other loots will be discarded.
Max loot in the carousel/to be saved can come from quest/hero passives/tavern upgrades/Contract Plan.

acceptance.text: Show all pending loot in a browseable choice surface before the player commits.
Let the player select up to the current recovery capacity, then confirm that unselected loot is discarded.
Prevent keeping every pending item through repeated Bring this back actions when capacity is lower than the drop count.
Keep recovery capacity sources compatible with quest, hero passive, facility, and contract-plan bonuses.
Add or update focused tests for grouped pending-loot selection and discard behavior.

notes: 2026-06-25: Started implementation; treating this as a grouped pending-loot selection flow with confirmation.

2026-06-25: Reward Loot now uses a grouped browse/select/confirm flow; selected loot is committed atomically up to recovery capacity and unselected pending loot is discarded. Tests, assembleDebug, lintDebug, and diff check passed.

tasks.title: New task
New items

We need to create new items. New rule is that 1 item = 1 artwork. All existing items will be reassigned to the common rarity. 
We will create 8 items per other rarity level, with corresponding name, artwork, stats etc etc. Refer to the loot excel sheet items.csv

Add 8 Uncommon, 8 Rare, 8 Epic, and 8 Relic item definitions.

Update docs/data/items.csv and the hero/item catalog to mirror accepted rarity, art, and stat rules.
Add tests or checks that prevent placeholder/fallback art from being accepted as final item artwork.

notes: 2026-06-25: Started implementation after pushing BV-005. Scope: fixed-rarity item catalog, 8 items per non-common rarity, and one artwork reference per item while preserving legacy LootIcon snapshots.

tasks.title: Define real loot art quality bar and item brief template

acceptance.text: Define minimum acceptable item-art format, dimensions, transparency/background rules, and in-game readability constraints.
Define rarity visual language for Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, and Relic without relying on recolored generic shapes.
Create a per-item brief template that includes name, slot, rarity, silhouette, material, joke/readability hook, and forbidden shortcuts.

notes: This exists to prevent placeholder/vector geometry from being accepted as artwork again.
